content:
# Joshua Chapter 8 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), folio pages 231–232 | Codex Vaticanus (LXX)
**Source Image:** `JOS_008_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_008_page_002.jpg`

---

## YHWH Restores Joshua and Commands the Ambush (vv. 1–8)

**8:1** And YHWH said to Joshua, "Do not fear and do not be dismayed. Take with you all the people of war and arise, go up to Ai. See, I have given into your hand the king of Ai, and his people, and his city, and his land.

**8:2** And you shall do to Ai and to its king as you did to Jericho and to its king. Only its spoil and its livestock you shall plunder for yourselves. Set for yourself an ambush against the city from behind it."

**8:3** And Joshua arose, and all the people of war, to go up to Ai. And Joshua chose thirty thousand men, mighty men of valor, and he sent them by night.

**8:4** And he commanded them, saying, "See, you are to lie in ambush against the city, from behind the city. Do not go very far from the city, and all of you be ready.

**8:5** And I and all the people who are with me will approach the city. And it shall be, when they come out to meet us as at the first, that we will flee before them.

**8:6** And they will come out after us until we have drawn them away from the city, for they will say, 'They are fleeing before us as at the first.' And we will flee before them.

**8:7** Then you shall rise up from the ambush and take possession of the city, and YHWH your God will give it into your hand.

**8:8** And it shall be, when you have seized the city, that you shall set the city on fire. According to the word of YHWH you shall do. See, I have commanded you."

---

## The Ambush Is Set (vv. 9–13)

**8:9** And Joshua sent them, and they went to the place of ambush and stayed between Bethel and Ai, on the west side of Ai. And Joshua lodged that night in the midst of the people.

**8:10** And Joshua rose early in the morning and mustered the people, and he went up — he and the elders of Israel — before the people to Ai.

**8:11** And all the people of war who were with him went up and drew near and came before the city, and they camped on the north side of Ai. And the valley was between them and Ai.

**8:12** And he took about five thousand men and set them as an ambush between Bethel and Ai, on the west side of the city.

**8:13** And they stationed the people — all the camp that was on the north side of the city, and its rear guard on the west side of the city. And Joshua went that night into the midst of the valley.

---

## The Battle of Ai (vv. 14–23)

**8:14** And it was, when the king of Ai saw it, that the men of the city hurried and rose early and went out to meet Israel for battle — he and all his people — at the appointed place before the Arabah. And he did not know that there was an ambush against him behind the city.

**8:15** And Joshua and all Israel let themselves be struck before them, and they fled by the way of the wilderness.

**8:16** And all the people who were in Ai were called together to pursue after them, and they pursued after Joshua and were drawn away from the city.

**8:17** And there was not a man left in Ai or in Bethel who had not gone out after Israel. And they left the city open and pursued after Israel.

**8:18** And YHWH said to Joshua, "Stretch out the javelin that is in your hand toward Ai, for I will give it into your hand." And Joshua stretched out the javelin that was in his hand toward the city.

**8:19** And the ambush arose quickly from its place, and they ran as soon as he stretched out his hand, and they entered the city and captured it. And they hurried and set the city on fire.

**8:20** And the men of Ai turned behind them and looked, and behold, the smoke of the city ascended to the heavens, and they had no power to flee this way or that way. And the people who had been fleeing toward the wilderness turned back against the pursuers.

**8:21** And Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had captured the city, and that the smoke of the city ascended. And they turned back and struck the men of Ai.

**8:22** And these came out from the city to meet them, and they were in the midst of Israel — some on this side and some on that side. And they struck them until there was not left to them a survivor or a fugitive.

**8:23** And the king of Ai they captured alive and brought him near to Joshua.

---

## The Destruction of Ai (vv. 24–29)

**8:24** And it was, when Israel had finished slaying all the inhabitants of Ai in the field, in the wilderness where they had pursued them, and all of them had fallen by the edge of the sword until they were consumed, that all Israel returned to Ai and struck it with the edge of the sword.

**8:25** And all who fell on that day, both men and women, were twelve thousand — all the people of Ai.

**8:26** And Joshua did not draw back his hand with which he stretched out the javelin until he had devoted to destruction all the inhabitants of Ai.

**8:27** Only the livestock and the spoil of that city did Israel plunder for themselves, according to the word of YHWH that he commanded Joshua.

**8:28** And Joshua burned Ai and made it a mound forever, a desolation unto this day.

**8:29** And the king of Ai he hanged on a tree until the time of evening. And at the going down of the sun, Joshua commanded, and they took down his body from the tree and cast it at the entrance of the gate of the city. And they raised over it a great heap of stones, unto this day.

---

## The Altar at Mount Ebal and the Reading of the Torah (vv. 30–35)

**8:30** Then Joshua built an altar to YHWH, the God of Israel, on Mount Ebal,

**8:31** just as Moses the servant of YHWH had commanded the Sons of Israel, as it is written in the book of the Torah of Moses: "An altar of whole stones upon which no one has wielded iron." And they offered upon it burnt offerings to YHWH and sacrificed peace offerings.

**8:32** And he wrote there upon the stones a copy of the Torah of Moses, which he had written before the Sons of Israel.

**8:33** And all Israel, and their elders, and officers, and their judges, stood on this side and on that side of the Ark, before the Levitical priests who carried the Ark of the Covenant of YHWH — the sojourner as well as the native-born — half of them in front of Mount Gerizim and half of them in front of Mount Ebal, just as Moses the servant of YHWH had commanded at the first, to bless the people of Israel.

**8:34** And afterward he read all the words of the Torah, the blessing and the curse, according to all that is written in the book of the Torah.

**8:35** There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded that Joshua did not read before all the assembly of Israel, and the women, and the little ones, and the sojourners who walked in their midst.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

SOPHIA evaluated all thirty-five verses of Joshua 8 across the three translator renderings (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA). The Leningrad Codex (folio pages 231–232) and Codex Vaticanus were consulted throughout. The chapter divides into two major sections: the conquest of Ai by ambush (vv. 1–29) and the covenant ceremony at Mount Ebal (vv. 30–35).

**Manuscript observations:** The Leningrad Codex pages 231–232 contain the full text of Joshua 8 in three columns of clear scribal hand with Masoretic vowel pointing and cantillation marks. The Vaticanus LXX page shows the Greek text of Joshua 8, which diverges from the MT most notably in v. 3 (reading three thousand instead of thirty thousand) and in the placement of vv. 30–35 (which the LXX positions after 9:2). The consensus follows the MT throughout.

**General principles applied:** (1) Preserve "And" at the start of verses per binding convention for Hebrew conjunctions. (2) Maintain "Sons of Israel" for bene Yisra'el. (3) Use "YHWH" transliterated throughout. (4) Numbers rendered in English words ("thirty thousand," "five thousand," "twelve thousand"). (5) Prefer formal register that preserves Hebrew structure while producing clear English. (6) "Torah" retained rather than "law" or "instruction." (7) "Sojourner" for ger, preserving the specific legal category. (8) "Ark" capitalized throughout.

**Key decisions:**

1. **Vv. 1–8 (YHWH's command):** High agreement among all three translators. The consensus follows LOGOS closely, with EMET confirming Hebrew fidelity. PNEUMA's dynamic renderings ("Do not lose heart," "march against," "Listen carefully") were set aside in favor of closer formal equivalents.

2. **V. 3 (thirty thousand / three thousand):** All three translators follow the MT's thirty thousand (sheloshim eleph). The LXX Vaticanus reads three thousand (trischilious), possibly harmonizing with 7:4. The consensus follows the MT. The relationship between the thirty thousand of v. 3 and the five thousand of v. 12 remains textually uncertain — they may represent two separate forces or a whole and a subset.

3. **V. 13 ("its rear guard"):** EMET renders aqevo as "its rear" (literally "its heel"); LOGOS and PNEUMA both use "rear guard." The consensus follows "rear guard" as it captures the military sense of the Hebrew in context while remaining close to the literal meaning.

4. **V. 15 ("let themselves be struck"):** EMET renders the Niphal va-yinnag'u as "were struck" (passive, ambiguous); LOGOS renders "let themselves be struck" (tolerative Niphal); PNEUMA renders "let themselves be beaten back." The consensus follows LOGOS, which captures the deliberate feigned-defeat nuance of the Niphal without over-interpreting.

5. **V. 18 ("javelin" — kidon):** All three translators agree on "javelin" for kidon, supported by the LXX's gaison. The outstretched posture described in v. 26 confirms a weapon used as a visible signal, not a short sword.

6. **V. 20 ("and behold"):** EMET and LOGOS preserve ve-hinneh as "and behold"; PNEUMA omits it. The consensus retains "and behold" as it marks a moment of dramatic realization in the Hebrew narrative.

7. **V. 26 ("devoted to destruction"):** EMET uses "devoted"; LOGOS uses "devoted to destruction"; PNEUMA uses "devoted to destruction." The consensus follows the fuller rendering to preserve the theological weight of the cherem concept (Hiphil of charam).

8. **V. 28 ("a mound forever"):** EMET renders tel olam as "a mound of perpetuity"; LOGOS as "a mound forever"; PNEUMA as "a permanent ruin." The consensus follows LOGOS — "mound" preserves the specific archaeological term (tel), and "forever" renders olam more naturally than "of perpetuity" while avoiding PNEUMA's departure from the Hebrew tel.

9. **V. 29 ("body" vs. "corpse"):** EMET uses "corpse" (nivlato); LOGOS uses "body"; PNEUMA uses "body." The Hebrew nivlah specifically means "corpse" or "carcass," but "body" is acceptable in context since the man is already dead. The consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA with "body" for readability, though "corpse" would be more precise.

10. **Vv. 30–35 (Ebal ceremony):** LOGOS and PNEUMA agree on most phrasing. EMET preserves the article on "the-Ai" throughout (reflecting ha-Ai in Hebrew) and uses "scroll" for sefer where LOGOS/PNEUMA use "book." The consensus uses "book" (the standard English rendering of sefer) and drops the article on Ai (treating it as a proper name in English despite the Hebrew article). The LXX places this section after 9:2; the consensus follows the MT placement.

11. **V. 31 ("Torah" vs. "law"):** EMET and LOGOS use "Torah"; PNEUMA uses "law." The consensus retains "Torah" per established SOPHIA convention, consistent with Joshua 1.

12. **V. 33 ("sojourner" vs. "foreigners"):** EMET and LOGOS use "sojourner" for ger; PNEUMA uses "foreigners." The consensus follows "sojourner," which preserves the specific legal and social category of the ger in Israelite law, distinct from nokri (foreigner).

Full verse-by-verse comparison of all disagreements is recorded in `disagreements/JOS_008.md`.

---

## Disagreement Log

*See `disagreements/JOS_008.md` for the complete disagreement log.*
